indexes.usage: Load entire zone map via fread (4808 bytes, L1-resident). Linear scan of 600 entries to find contiguous qualifying block window [block_lo, block_hi). Compute row_lo = block_lo * block_size, row_hi = min(block_hi * block_size, total_rows).

data_structures.zone_map: flat_array(600 entries, loaded via fread, L1-resident)
data_structures.column_buffers: 4 anonymous heap buffers (posix_memalign 64-byte aligned) sized to qualifying row range only (~16.8MB total); populated via pread, no mmap, no page fault overhead
data_structures.thread_accumulator: double per-thread, stored in cache-line-padded struct (64-byte alignment) to prevent false sharing across 64 threads

parallelism.phase1_io: 4 threads, one per column, each issues a single pread() call for the qualifying row range of its column; all 4 run concurrently to overlap column I/O
parallelism.phase2_scan: 64 threads partition [row_lo, row_hi) evenly; each thread scans its sub-range over the in-memory buffers with no I/O or page fault overhead

io_strategy.rationale: mmap requires one kernel-mode page fault per 4KB page to install PTE even when page is in OS cache (~2-5us/page × 4200 pages = 8-21ms). pread() copies directly from page cache to pre-allocated anonymous memory at memory bandwidth (~20 GB/s), costing ~0.84ms for 16.8MB total. Eliminates the dominant bottleneck from all prior iterations.

inner_loop.description: Per-row loop over in-memory buffers (zero I/O in this phase)
inner_loop.note: All pointers are to base of qualifying buffer (offset 0 = row_lo). Use local index j = i - row_lo for buffer access, or adjust loop bounds to j in [0, count). Per-block zone check still applied — skip any block where zone.mx < LO || zone.mn >= HI (handles boundary blocks correctly).

output.mkdir_strategy: direct_syscall: use mkdir() with errno==EEXIST check instead of system('mkdir -p ...'). Eliminates 3-5ms fork+exec overhead.

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category B — algorithm change): all prior iterations use mmap for the column files. Even on hot runs where data is in the OS page cache, mmap requires a kernel-mode page fault per 4KB page to install the PTE into the process page table (~2–5µs/page). The qualifying row range spans ~4200 pages (16.8MB across 4 columns), giving 8–21ms of page-fault overhead — confirmed by iter_4's profiling showing partial_mmap_populate=10ms and main_scan=6ms (total 16ms measured, 20ms wall). The fix replaces mmap entirely with pread() into pre-allocated anonymous buffers: (1) load zone map via fread (4808 bytes, unchanged); (2) compute qualifying row range [row_lo, row_hi) from zone scan; (3) posix_memalign 4 buffers for the qualifying window (~16.8MB total, fits in 44MB L3); (4) 4 concurrent pread() threads — one per column — to fill buffers from page cache at ~20 GB/s (~0.84ms total); (5) 64-thread parallel scan over in-memory buffers with zero page-fault overhead; (6) reduce + output with direct mkdir() syscall instead of system(). Anonymous memory does not require PTE installation via kernel VFS, making the entire 4200-page working set instantly accessible after pread completes.
